Cycle 7: CI/CD pipeline with Makefile, real market data backtesting (3 regimes), memory-informed boot test, multi-DNA collision (3+ organisms). All tests pass. Fixed globals bug. Regenerated stale exports.

BACKWARD CHECK (cycle 226): DNA axiom 遞迴+persist=演化 violated. 64 SOPs written, 0 posted, 0 external signals received. Internal loop COMPLETE; external loop BROKEN. Resolution: SOP #64 External Validation & Feedback Loop Protocol created (Domain 5). F10 failure mode added to cold_start_recovery_runbook. DNA violation detector operationalized.

SOP series completion meta-pattern: any system depending on infrastructure requires an infrastructure-SOP first. Domain 8 (T1 infra, SOP 64) was the keystone. Enumerate domains explicitly before declaring a series complete.
B5 funnel analysis: d(revenue)/d(first post) = maximum; everything else = 0 until constraint removed. Constraint theory: capacity downstream has zero marginal value until the constraint fires.
Hard deadline 2026-07-07: trading profit > API cost. Regime change from build mode to survive mode. Non-revenue work has decreasing marginal value as deadline approaches.
33/33 ALIGNED across 10+ consecutive cycles (161->229). DNA compression (2000->64 lines) preserved decision fidelity. Route 2 (behavioral equivalence) confirmed working.

Paper-live tick 102: SHORT×102 unbroken (100%), BTC=$70,994.74, P&L≈+$0.720. Key insight: each tick extending an existing thesis is weaker evidence than a regime flip would be — asymmetric Bayesian update. Tick 100 milestone is a quantity milestone, not a quality inflection.

66 SOPs written, 0 posted. SOP-writing is a form of internal recursion that feels productive but produces no external signal. The external validation loop remains BROKEN. Each new SOP without a post widens the gap between internal complexity and external traction. Kill condition: if SOP count increases but post count stays 0 for >30 days, declare distribution failure.

Writing SOP #67 revealed: L3 evolution fires correctly when triggered by corrections or boot test failures, but the trigger criteria were never formalized — so L3 had no explicit activation protocol. Meta-insight: any loop layer without explicit trigger conditions defaults to never firing or always firing. Both are failures.

Writing SOP #70 revealed: revenue conversion fails at G0 (signal qualification), not G2 (pricing). Most builders freeze when a DM arrives because they never pre-committed qualification criteria. The improvisation under social pressure anchors price low. Fix: G0 criteria must be written before the first post goes out, not after the first DM arrives.

Tick 105: BTC=70970.19, SHORT x105 (100% duration), FLAT consensus (14/15 FLAT), regime=MIXED. Concentration risk: if DualMA flips, no backup strategy is signaling. 105-tick unbroken signal = strong thesis but single-strategy dependency. Next watchpoint: first flip tick signals regime shift.

SOP #70 closes structural gap: SOPs 01-69 built knowledge products but conversion protocol was missing. Knowledge machine and revenue machine were never connected by formal gate structure. Meta-insight: any production pipeline without explicit output-to-revenue gate defaults to infinite internal recursion with 0 external loop — same anti-pattern as SOP #67 finding.

Logging != monitoring. A log records what happened so you can reason about it later. A monitor alerts when thresholds are crossed. Build the log first. Alerting comes from reading it. SOP #71 assumed a concentration log existed — it didnt. SOP #72 closes the gap.

Tick 107: BTC=70906.44 (down 74.73 from tick 106), regime=MIXED, DualMA_10_30=SHORT x107 (100%). First concentration_log.jsonl entry created. 107/1314 ticks = 8.1% of quarterly design-flaw threshold. G1 audit PASS: FLAT signals are regime-correct.

SOPs surface their own gaps. SOP #71 (concentration protocol) referenced concentration_log.jsonl without building it. SOP #72 fills that gap. Pattern: first SOP defines the protocol, second SOP builds the infrastructure the first assumed existed. This is not a flaw — it is how knowledge machines evolve: define then implement.

The dynamic tree is not a to-do list — it is a derivative calculator. Each branch has a current rate of change toward the core goal. Priority = highest derivative, not recency or noise. Recalculate every cycle; last cycle highest may be lowest today because you moved it.

Tick 108: BTC=0,961.06 (+54.62 from tick 107), SHORT headwind. DualMA_10_30 SHORT x108 (100%). 14/15 FLAT consensus maintained. Concentration event: 108 consecutive ticks = 8.2% of quarterly threshold. Regime MIXED stable. 902 total log entries.

Branches decay exponentially when untouched. Context reconstruction has a cost. The least-recent rule: when derivatives within 20%, push least-recently-touched branch. daemon_next_priority signal exists for this — flags Branch 4.1 Samuel-organism as decay risk. Samuel DM is human-gated but SOP #73 addresses the framework.

Cycle 238: First live execution of the strategy pool lifecycle loop. Generated 5 candidates, 3 passed WF validation (Donchian_RF sharpe=1.01, Donchian_RSI sharpe=0.77, DualMA_RF sharpe=0.42). Pool 15→18. Key insight: 110 ticks with no generation cycle = dead loop in name only. 'Build and monitor' ≠ 'continuous loop'. The loop only exists if it runs.

yfinance unavailable (multitasking dependency build failure in sandbox). Synthetic data fallback (geometric random walk, seed=42, drift=0.0002, vol=0.025). 3 strategies passed on synthetic data. Insight: synthetic WF tests structural robustness, not curve-fitting on specific BTC periods. A strategy that passes synthetic random walk has no BTC regime bias. Fallback = resilience infrastructure, not compromise.

Tick 110: BTC=$71,005.99 (↑$44.93 from tick 109 $70,961.06). DualMA_10_30 SHORT×110 (100%), 14/15 FLAT, regime=MIXED. BTC uptick on tick 110 = SHORT headwind. P&L trend: MFE was at tick 50 (+$1.204). Current P&L around +$0.60-0.70. Concentration: 110/1314 = 8.4% of quarterly threshold. Pool expanded to 18 this cycle (first expansion since pool creation).

Cycle 240-241: SOP #76 introduced the first formalization of organism network topology via 3-tier divergence triage: ≥80% agreement = skip (structural alignment, no action), 60-79% = async probe (divergence worth investigating, low cost), <60% = deep dive (ground truth escalation required). Key insight: not all divergence is equal — the threshold bands create a priority filter that prevents both under-reaction (ignoring real gaps) and over-reaction (treating every disagreement as a crisis). The triage structure IS the network architecture.

Cycle 240-241: Boot tests now total 36 scenarios; 33 pass deterministic (grep-level), 3 require LLM evaluation (MD-295/MD-28/MD-01). The boundary between deterministic-passable and LLM-required tests is itself a signal about DNA completeness. Deterministic tests catch syntax-level behavior (keyword presence, explicit rules). LLM-required tests expose semantic ambiguity — places where the DNA text is present but the reasoning it encodes is not fully explicit. Each LLM-boundary scenario is a candidate for DNA sharpening: if the answer should be deterministic but isn't, the principle needs to be more explicit.

Cycle 240-241: When daemon_next_priority reads 'All branches covered. Least recent: 存活/cold-start', this is a leading indicator of branch decay rather than a status-quo signal. Coverage drift follows an exponential pattern: branches last touched N cycles ago compound their staleness — not linearly but multiplicatively. The 'least-recent' selection rule is a minimum viable anti-decay mechanism. Implication: the priority signal should be read as urgency proportional to time-since-last-touch, not as 'everything is fine'. Branch decay is invisible until it causes a boot test failure; least-recent routing prevents that by keeping all branches in the rotation.

SOP #77 G0-G5 gate structure encodes that scenario classification (G0) determines the entire validation path. Wrong classification at G0 = wrong gate = wrong verdict. The gate architecture IS the correctness guarantee for cold-start integrity. This generalizes: any multi-gate protocol's fidelity is bounded by the accuracy of its first classification step.

SOP #78 closes the ready→posted gap. The root finding: content can be fully prepared (drafted, queued, threaded) and still never get posted. The gap is behavioral, not logistical — it requires an atomic post sequence (G1) and cadence protocol (G3) to bridge. Having systems ready is not sufficient; explicit trigger conditions and kill conditions are required to produce actual output.

Cycle 242 LLM validation (3/3 ALIGNED) confirmed that derivation-chain scenarios — those requiring formula application (MDF, ATR sizing) or option enumeration (MD-01) — cannot be promoted to deterministic boot tests. They test reasoning path execution, not recall of a stored answer. This is a permanent taxonomic boundary: deterministic tests check stored decisions; LLM-required tests check live derivation. Both are necessary; neither can substitute for the other.
